pattern: Two fixed indices plus a two-pointer sweep generalise 3Sum to find all distinct quadruplets reaching the target, with duplicate skips at every level.
hints: Pin the two smallest indices, then the remaining work is the same closing two-pointer search used for pair sums.
Sort first so equal values are adjacent; you can then skip repeats at each of the four positions to avoid duplicate quadruplets.
Sort, loop i and j (skipping repeated values), and for each pair run l=j+1, r=n-1 inward toward target, recording matches and skipping duplicate l/r values after a hit.

edge_inputs.description: mixed signs with three distinct quadruplets
five identical values, only one quadruplet
fewer than four elements yields nothing
exactly four elements that hit the target
large magnitudes that sum without overflow in 64-bit

stress.description: array over a small value range with target zero to maximise matching quadruplets
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(4, min(size, 120))
    nums = [rng.randrange(-20, 21) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums, 0]
stress.note: narrow range and target 0 produce many duplicate-skipped quadruplets
